title: StorageClass Reclaim Policies
description:
## StorageClass Reclaim Policies

A PersistentVolumeClaim bound to a PV is deleted. The StorageClass has `reclaimPolicy: Retain`.

What happens to the underlying PersistentVolume?

options.text: The PV is immediately deleted along with all data
The PV remains but enters a `Released` state; the data is preserved and must be manually reclaimed
The PV is automatically rebound to the next PVC that requests the same storage class
The PV is converted to an emptyDir volume attached to the node

explanation: With `reclaimPolicy: Retain`, when a PVC is deleted the PV moves to `Released` state — the data is **preserved** on the storage backend, but the PV is not available for automatic rebinding. An admin must manually reclaim it (delete the PV, clean the storage, recreate the PV). Contrast with `reclaimPolicy: Delete`, which deletes both the PV object and the underlying storage asset automatically.

hints.title: Reclaim policies
hints.body: Three policies exist: `Retain` (manual reclaim), `Delete` (auto-delete storage), `Recycle` (deprecated, basic scrub). Check with: `kubectl get storageclass`.
